title: Table for One
description: Test your understanding of the video and practise useful restaurant vocabulary, meal words and food expressions.

questions.question: What is a “reservation”?
options: A table or place booked in advance
A small dish before the meal
questions.answer: A table or place booked in advance
questions.explanation: Sune has a reservation, meaning a table has been booked for him in advance.

questions.question: What is a “maître d’”?
options: The person who welcomes guests and shows them to their table
The person who cooks the food
The person who washes the dishes
The person who pays the bill
questions.answer: The person who welcomes guests and shows them to their table
questions.explanation: The maître d’ greets Sune at the entrance and shows him to his table.

questions.question: What is a “menu”?
options: The small stick for your teeth
questions.explanation: Sune picks up the menu to see what food he can order.

questions.question: What is a “starter”?
options: A small dish before the main meal
The biggest dish of the meal
questions.answer: A small dish before the main meal
questions.explanation: A starter is a small dish you eat before the main course.

questions.question: What does “on the side” mean?
questions.explanation: Sune asks for bread on the side, meaning separately as an extra.

questions.question: What is a “tablecloth”?
options: The cloth that covers a table
questions.answer: The cloth that covers a table
questions.explanation: The candle flickers on the tablecloth, the cloth covering the table.

questions.question: What is “steam”?
options: Hot vapour that rises from hot food or liquid
The sound of a waiter writing
questions.answer: Hot vapour that rises from hot food or liquid
questions.explanation: The plate arrives steaming hot, with steam rising from the food.

questions.question: What does “get stuck in” mean?
options: To start doing something with enthusiasm
To wait for a long time
questions.answer: To start doing something with enthusiasm
questions.explanation: Sune gets stuck in when he starts eating enthusiastically.

questions.question: What does “mop up” mean in the video?
options: To use bread to soak up the remaining sauce
To clean the floor with water
To cut food into small pieces
questions.answer: To use bread to soak up the remaining sauce
questions.explanation: Sune mops up the sauce with bread from the bread basket.

sections.title: Video comprehension
sections.instructions: Answer the questions about the story.

questions.question: Where does Sune go at the beginning of the video?
questions.explanation: Sune walks into the restaurant because he has a reservation.

questions.question: What kind of table does Sune get?
questions.explanation: Sune is shown to his table: a table for one.

questions.question: What does Sune order?
options: Pasta and bread on the side
questions.answer: Pasta and bread on the side
questions.explanation: Sune orders pasta and asks for some bread on the side.

questions.question: What does Sune do with the sauce?
options: He mops it up with bread
He spills it on the tablecloth
He leaves it on the plate
questions.answer: He mops it up with bread
questions.explanation: Sune uses bread to soak up the remaining sauce on the plate.

questions.question: How does Sune feel after seeing the bill?
questions.explanation: Sune stares at the total, and his eyes say everything.

sections.title: Complete the sentence
sections.instructions: Choose the correct word or expression.

questions.question: Could I have the pasta, please? And some bread ______?
questions.explanation: On the side means served separately as an extra.

questions.question: The food was so good that Sune ______ every last bit.
questions.explanation: To wolf something down means to eat it very quickly and hungrily.

questions.question: At the end of the meal, the waiter brings the ______.
questions.explanation: The bill, also called the receipt or the check, shows how much you have to pay.
